Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

question:
Will turnout in the first round of the 2025 Chilean presidential election be between 82% and 84%?

Description: First-round presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Chile on November 16, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the first-round turnout percentage, calculated as “Total votación” divided by “Total inscritos.”

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the results of the election aren’t known by March 31, 2026 ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.  

This market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Chilean Government, specifically the Chilean Electoral Service (Servicio Electoral de Chile, Serval) (https://www.servel.cl/).

label: NO
rationale: Base rate: Chilean first-round presidential turnout has historically been well below 82% even before considering that this is a specific by-date forecast; 2013 was 46.72%, 2017 was 47.33%, and 2021 was 47.46% ([mkt_681515_doc_010], [mkt_681515_doc_004]). The main factor pushing turnout higher is the return of mandatory voting for the 2025 presidential election, which official Servel information confirms will apply to this election ([mkt_681515_doc_017]); however, the evidence does not show that mandatory voting will reliably produce turnout in the 82%-84% band. A recent Chile example of mandatory participation, the 2020 referendum, reached 50.95% turnout ([mkt_681515_doc_010]), and historical discussion notes mandatory referendums have risen higher than 80% in some cases, but that is indirect and not specific to this presidential election ([mkt_681515_doc_005]). Current pre-election coverage and polls indicate a competitive race but do not provide concrete turnout evidence ([mkt_681515_doc_003], [mkt_681515_doc_009], [mkt_681515_doc_002]). Overall, mandatory voting materially raises turnout from the old ~47% presidential baseline, but 82%-84% is still a very high bar, so the probability remains well below 0.5.

Pluralization/possessive of the word will count toward the resolution of this market.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

question:
Will the Fed cut-pause-cut in 2025

Description: The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve according to the decisions made by the next three Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings in 2025 (September 16-17, October 28-29, and December 9-10).

A qualifying cut occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is lower compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying hike occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is higher compared to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

A qualifying pause occurs when the new upper bound of the target federal funds rate is equal to the level it was prior to the respective meeting.

If the Fed publishes a different combination than any listed, this market will resolve to "Other".

Emergency rate cuts outside the regularly scheduled meetings will not be considered. 

Any rate hike will be encompassed by "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the FOMC’s statement after its meetings: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/fomccalendars.htm

The level and change of the target federal funds rate is also published at the official website of the Federal Reserve: https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm

If no statement is released for the December meeting by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

label: YES
rationale: Base rate for a specific 3-meeting sequence like cut-pause-cut is well below 50%, but the concrete evidence materially raises it. [mkt_587217_doc_005] and [mkt_587217_doc_007] show the September 16–17, 2025 FOMC meeting already occurred and the Fed lowered the target range by 25 bps to 4.00%-4.25%, so the first leg (cut) is locked in. [mkt_587217_doc_001] reports the September 2025 projections, with a substantial share of participants clustered around lower 2025 midpoint levels, consistent with at least one more cut in the remaining meetings. [mkt_587217_doc_011] says the median member expected an additional 50 bps of cuts over the remaining two meetings in 2025, which is supportive of a cut-pause-cut path only if one meeting pauses in between. However, this is still indirect because projections are not decisions, and the market requires exactly cut, then pause, then cut at the next three scheduled meetings. The main uncertainty is whether the October meeting was a pause and whether December delivered the second cut; the provided evidence does not confirm those outcomes. On balance, the already-confirmed September cut plus Fed projections of further easing make YES more likely than the base rate, but not overwhelmingly so.

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 18 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

label: YES
rationale: Base rate for a specific day’s NYC high staying at or below 41°F in mid-November is below 50%, but November 18 is late enough in the season that sub-42°F highs are plausible. The strongest evidence is that Weather Underground’s LaGuardia Airport Station history page is the resolution source and currently shows a very mild recent/high value of 84°F on the NYC weather history page, which is not directly predictive but confirms the station/source context [mkt_682604_doc_001]. NOAA/NWS historical data confirm LaGuardia is the correct long-running official station used for climate records, so the market is anchored to a reliable airport observation site [mkt_682604_doc_006]. Seasonal outlook material points to a generally milder-than-normal November in the Northeast, which pushes somewhat against an extreme cold high, but those are broad and weak signals rather than day-specific evidence [mkt_682604_doc_003] [mkt_682604_doc_002]. Overall, with no concrete forecast yet for Nov 18, I shrink toward the base rate but keep a modest YES lean because by mid/late November NYC does sometimes top out at 41°F or lower.

question:
Will Dell say "Confidence" during earnings call?

Description: This market will resolve based on the next earnings announcement of Dell currently scheduled to take place on November 25, 2025 at 4:00 PM EST.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source will be audio of the event.

label: YES
rationale: Base rate for a specific word-mention on an earnings call is below 50%, but Dell’s earnings transcripts repeatedly feature forward-looking language around AI demand, growth, and management commentary, making a brief mention of "confidence" somewhat likely. The strongest concrete evidence is that Dell has used the term in prior earnings-related materials: the Bloomberg clip includes a Dell discussion where "confidence" is spoken in the context of capital/liquidity and market confidence ([mkt_647171_doc_006]); Dell’s Q1 2026 transcript shows management discussing strong pipeline, shipment growth, and demand dynamics, a style of Q&A where a speaker may naturally use the word "confidence" ([mkt_647171_doc_009]); and Dell’s Q2 2026 earnings call transcript indicates an active, detailed earnings Q&A environment with management and analysts discussing record results and raised guidance ([mkt_647171_doc_017], [mkt_647171_doc_013]). However, these are indirect and do not prove the exact term will appear on the next call, so the estimate is only moderately above the base rate.

question:
Will Hinge Health (HNGE) beat quarterly earnings?

Description: Hinge Health is estimated to release earnings on November 4, 2025. The Street consensus estimate for Hinge Health's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $-0.21. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Hinge Health reports GAAP EPS greater than $-0.21 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Hinge Health releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.

Resolution source: https://seekingalpha.com/

label: NO
rationale: Base rate is below 50% because beating a quarterly GAAP EPS consensus requires a specific, timely earnings outcome, and Hinge Health has recently shown very large GAAP losses. [mkt_656723_doc_020] reports Q2 2025 GAAP net loss of $575.65 million, or -$13.10 per share, indicating the company’s GAAP earnings are still deeply negative. The only direct timing evidence is that Hinge Health has announced it will report Q3 2025 results after market close on the expected date, so the earnings release itself appears likely to occur on time. [mkt_656723_doc_016] However, the more relevant forward-looking context points to only modest improvement: a preview citing consensus Q3 2025 EPS of -$0.15 per share versus the market’s threshold of greater than -$0.21. [mkt_656723_doc_010] That means the market is asking for an outcome that is better than consensus, but still negative; while possible, there is no concrete evidence here that Hinge will outperform enough to clear the bar. Shrinking toward the base rate is appropriate because the evidence is indirect and does not show actual quarter results yet.

question:
Will between 340 and 349 house members vote “Yea” on an Epstein disclosure resolution/bill?

Description: U.S. Speaker of the House Mike Johnson stated that he plans to hold a vote next week on a measure mandating the release of Department of Justice files related to the sex trafficker Jeffrey Epstein. You can read more about that here: https://www.politico.com/live-updates/2025/11/12/congress/epstein-files-house-vote-next-week-00649999.

This market will resolve to the number of Members of the U.S. House of Representatives who vote “Yea” on the first House floor vote held on any bill, measure, or resolution which mandates, compels, or formally calls for the public release of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein.

The vote count refers to the first final vote on passage in the full chamber, not committee votes or procedural motions

If no such vote is held by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No vote by December 31”.
The resolution sources will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

label: YES
rationale: Base rate: House votes on high-profile disclosure measures can attract broad bipartisan support, but the exact band of 340-349 Yeas is still a fairly narrow target and requires both near-unanimous Democrats plus a large Republican crossover. question:
Will Gabriel Bortoleto win the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix pole?

Description: This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position at the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix, scheduled for October 26, 2025.

If the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after December 1, 2025, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2025 F1 Mexican Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA’s official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a driver sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that driver.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 26?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between November 14, and November 26, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question:
US x Venezuela military engagement by November 26?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if there is a military engagement between the military forces of the United States of America and Venezuela between November 14, and November 26, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A "military engagement" is defined as any incident involving the use of force such as missile strikes, artillery fire, exchange of gunfire, or other forms of direct military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces. Non-violent actions, such as warning shots or missile launches that land in territorial waters or pass through airspace, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution. Intentional ship ramming that results in significant damage to (e.g., a hole in the hull) or the sinking of a military ship by another will count toward a "Yes" resolution; however, minor damage (scrapes, dents) will not. 

Any U.S. military kinetic strike that impacts Venezuelan land territory will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

Missiles or drone strikes which are intercepted will not qualify regardless of whether they land on adversarial territory or cause damage. Similarly, surface-to-air missile strikes impacting adversarial territory will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution (although surface to air missiles fired at an adversarial military aircraft would qualify).

Note: the U.S. Coast Guard is a branch of the U.S. Armed Forces; Venezuela’s Milicia Nacional Bolivariana (militia) is a “special component” of the Venezuelan National Bolivarian Armed Forces (FANB); and Venezuela’s Coast Guard (Comando de Guardacostas) is a component of the Venezuelan Navy (Armada Bolivariana) and thus part of the FANB.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question:
Will Elon Musk post 500+ tweets from November 7 to November 14, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 7 12:00 PM ET to November 14, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

question:
Will Keir Starmer say "Mr. Speaker" 30+ times during the next Prime Minister's Questions?

Description: Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by February 28, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

label: YES
rationale: Base rate: PMQs is a recurring parliamentary event and Starmer typically speaks many times as the opposition leader, so the question is whether he will exceed 30 mentions of the phrase "Mr. Speaker" in a single live session. The evidence suggests this is plausible because PMQs lasts at least half an hour and involves many turns, with the Speaker calling members in sequence ([mkt_678599_doc_018], [mkt_678599_doc_010]). The key live evidence is a recent PMQs broadcast page showing Starmer is participating in PMQs on 12 Nov 2025, confirming the event format and that his remarks are part of the live broadcast stream ([mkt_678599_doc_004]). However, none of the sources directly count his exact usage of "Mr. Speaker," so this remains an inference from the structure of PMQs rather than direct proof. Given the usual repetitive address format in PMQs, 30+ uses is somewhat likely but not certain.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be 65°F or higher on November 26?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Nov '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of November 2025.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

question:
Will Meta reach $870 in November?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during November 2025 (ET), any 1-minute candle for Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during regular trading hours (ET) will be considered.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) "High" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/META/, with the chart settings on "1m" for candle intervals.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/META/

Analyst consensus cited in the forecast article is below the target at roughly $829.66 average with a range up to $1,086 ([mkt_657162_doc_010]), which leaves upside possible but does not itself indicate the stock is on track to touch $870 in November. The remaining materials are generic bullish commentary about ad growth/AI spending ([mkt_657162_doc_001], [mkt_657162_doc_006], [mkt_657162_doc_015]) and do not provide dated evidence that META is already approaching $870 during November. question:
Deebo Samuel Sr.: Anytime Touchdown

Description: In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for November 30 at 8:20 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Deebo Samuel Sr. scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Deebo Samuel Sr. does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

Resolution source: https://www.nfl.com/scores

label: YES

label: NO
rationale: Base rate: Fed governors are rarely removed or resign on a short deadline, so absent strong evidence the default is NO. The strongest concrete evidence cuts against an actual departure by Nov. 30: the Federal Reserve’s November 2025 calendar still lists Governor Lisa D. Cook giving speeches on Nov. 4 and Nov. 20, indicating she remained in office at least through those dates ([mkt_657493_doc_001], [mkt_657493_doc_007], [mkt_657493_doc_013]). The Fed’s official members page and speech pages are high-credibility government sources and are inconsistent with a completed resignation/removal during the market window. There is also a legal/political fight over Trump’s attempted removal: Trump sent a dismissal letter on Aug. 25 ([mkt_657493_doc_002]), but later reporting says an appeals panel denied Trump’s bid to fire Cook and that Cook made a strong showing the attempt violated federal law ([mkt_657493_doc_012]); SCOTUSblog reports Cook asked the Supreme Court to let her remain in office while the challenge continued ([mkt_657493_doc_009]) and that the administration sought permission to fire her ([mkt_657493_doc_016]). Congressional Record remarks likewise describe an attempt to fire her unless she resigns, not confirmation that she actually left ([mkt_657493_doc_017], [mkt_657493_doc_018]). Because the available evidence points to an attempted but not completed removal, I keep the probability below 0.5 and near a low base rate.
question:
Lisa Cook out as Fed Governor by November 30?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if Lisa Cook announces her resignation or otherwise ceases to be a member of the Federal Reserve Board of Governors for any period of time between August 25, and November 30, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Only official announcements of Cook's resignation or removal before her term is scheduled to end, made by either Cook or the Board of Governors, will qualify. Announcements from Trump or his administration will not alone qualify.

Temporary absences or attempts at termination which do not actually remove Cook from the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve will not qualify. 

The resolution source for this market will be information from the U.S. Government however a consensus of credible reporting will also suffice.

label: NO
rationale: Base rate: questions about whether a Fed governor is out by a near-term deadline usually resolve NO unless there is a clear official resignation/removal or an effective court/board action. The strongest dated evidence is that President Trump issued a dismissal letter on August 25, 2025 [mkt_657493_doc_002], but the market rules say Trump/admin announcements alone do not qualify. Subsequent evidence cuts against an actual removal: on September 2, 2025, the Congressional Record states Lisa Cook "is a member of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve" and describes only an attempt to fire her if she does not retire or resign [mkt_657493_doc_017, mkt_657493_doc_018]. On September 18 and September 25, 2025, SCOTUSblog reports that the Trump administration asked the Supreme Court to allow her firing and that Cook urged the Court to let her remain in office while her challenge continues, indicating the issue was still being litigated rather than resolved [mkt_657493_doc_016, mkt_657493_doc_009]. Most importantly, a Federal Reserve speech page dated November 3, 2025 refers to "Governor Lisa D. Cook" giving remarks, showing she was still serving during the market window [mkt_657493_doc_007]. A Brookings/YouTube listing also shows a November 3–4, 2025 event featuring Fed Governor Lisa Cook [mkt_657493_doc_019]. Taken together, the evidence indicates she was still a Governor through the key period, so the probability of a qualifying resignation/removal before November 30 is low.

Description: This market will resolve to the number of senators who vote “Yea” on the first U.S. Senate floor vote held on any bill, measure, or resolution which mandates, compels, or formally calls for the public release of documents related to Jeffrey Epstein.

The vote count refers to the first final vote on passage in the full chamber, not committee votes or procedural motions

If no such vote is held by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No vote by December 31”.

The resolution sources will be official congressional voting records and a consensus of credible reporting.

label: NO
rationale: Base rate is low: a Senate floor vote on a specific disclosure bill ending in the narrow 75-79 Yea band is uncommon and requires a fairly exact bipartisan coalition. The strongest evidence is [mkt_687514_doc_001], which shows the Senate already considered an Epstein-related measure on Sept. 10, 2025 and the recorded vote was 51 Yea, 49 Nay on a motion to table an amendment "to direct the Attorney General to make publicly available documents related to Jeffrey Epstein"—well below the 75-79 range. [mkt_687514_doc_002] shows the Senate Epstein Files Transparency Act was introduced July 30, 2025, but that is only introduction and referral, not evidence of a final floor vote with supermajority-level support. [mkt_687514_doc_013] indicates the House later passed an Epstein files transparency bill, which makes a Senate vote more plausible, but it does not show the Senate will produce a first final passage vote in the 75-79 band. Overall, there is evidence of Senate activity, but nothing concrete showing support has moved into the 75-79 range.

question:
Will the highest temperature in New York City be 47°F or higher on December 1?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the LaGuardia Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 1 Dec '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the LaGuardia Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ny/new-york-city/KLGA.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

The strongest concrete evidence is polling: several October 2025 surveys put Jeannette Jara around or above 20%, including 24% in Panel Ciudadano, 25.4% in Activa Research, 26% in Cadem, and roughly 12% in one Criteria snapshot that shows volatility rather than a settled consensus [mkt_666145_doc_001]. This is indirect evidence, and polls can miss late shifts, so I shrink toward the base rate rather than treating them as decisive. Overall, the evidence points to Jara being more likely than not to clear 20%, but with meaningful uncertainty, so I set the probability of under 20% at 32%.

question:
Will VVD win the fewest seats in the next Netherlands General Election?

Description: This market will resolve to the political party that wins the fewest number of seats among the listed parties in the Netherlands House of Representatives (Tweede Kamer der Staten-Generaal) as a result of this election.

Only listed parties will be considered. 

If voting in the Netherlands election for the House does not occur by July 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

In the case of a tie between two or more of the listed parties for the fewest seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation comes first in alphabetical order.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the listed party or coalition.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, it will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Electoral Council (Kiesraad) (https://www.kiesraad.nl/).

Note: If the GL/PvdA alliance splits and announces they will not run a joint list, this market will refer to the Labor Party (PvdA).

Even if polling shifts, the evidence does not show VVD on track to be last, so the probability should stay well below 0.5.

question:
Malik Washington: Anytime Touchdown

Description: In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for November 30 at 1:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Malik Washington scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Malik Washington does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

question:
Will Starbucks say "Comparable Sales" during earnings call?

Description: This market will resolve based on the next earnings announcement of Starbucks currently scheduled to take place on October 29, 2025 at 5:00 PM EDT.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone during this event. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A Q&A event will count toward the resolution of this market.

If this event is definitely cancelled, or otherwise is not aired by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Palantir Technologies Inc. (PLTR) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

Resolution source: https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history

label: NO
rationale: Base rate: a stock closing above an extremely high threshold in a single specified week is uncommon, so the prior is well below 0.5. The evidence provided does not include the actual Yahoo Finance price history or any dated market data showing PLTR is already near $172 by the relevant week. The closest concrete items are mixed/indirect: [mkt_690090_doc_014] says Palantir reported strong Q3 2025 results and raised guidance, which is positive for fundamentals but does not imply the stock will finish a specific week above $172; [mkt_690090_doc_011] notes Wedbush maintained an outperform rating with a $120 target, which is far below $172 and therefore argues against that level being the expected near-term close; [mkt_690090_doc_010] and [mkt_690090_doc_013] describe valuation concerns and analyst commentary around Palantir, reinforcing that a sustained move above such a high threshold is not clearly supported. Short-interest and options-skew references ([mkt_690090_doc_015], [mkt_690090_doc_017]) are indirect sentiment signals and not reliable evidence that the closing price will exceed $172 on the specified Friday. Overall, the available evidence is stale, indirect, and insufficient to move materially above the low base rate.

question:

Description: This market will resolve according to the iOS app, ranked #1 in the United States on the iPhone Apple App Store's overall Top Charts under "Free Apps", as of October 24, 2025, at 12:00 PM ET.

To find the overall chart, click "Apps" at the bottom of the US iOS App Store app, scroll down to "Top Free Apps" and click "See All". Then under "Free Apps" in the "Top Charts" section, you'll see the list that will be used as the resolution source to this market (https://apps.apple.com/us/charts/iphone).

question:
Will Elon Musk post 360-379 tweets from November 4 to November 11, 2025?

Description: This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from November 4 12:00 PM ET to November 11, 2025 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://www.xtracker.io/. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Resolution source: https://x.com/elonmusk

Description: The Goodreads Choice Awards are presented annually by Goodreads.com. For the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards, the winners will be announced on December 4, 2025.

This market will resolve according to the listed book that wins the Readers' Favorite Fantasy award in the 2025 Goodreads Choice Awards.

If, for any reason, no winner is declared by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the case of a tie for the winner, this market will resolve in favor of the listed book that comes first in alphabetical order.

The resolution source will be the Goodreads website (https://www.goodreads.com/choiceawards/best-books-2025); however, other credible reporting may be used.

Description: This market will resolve according to the iOS app, ranked #1 in the United States on the iPhone Apple App Store's overall Top Charts under “Paid Apps”, as of 12:00 PM ET on the specified date.

To find the overall chart, click “Apps” at the bottom of the US iOS App Store app, scroll down to “Top Paid Apps” and click “See All.” Then under “Paid Apps” in the “Top Charts” section, you’ll see the list that will be used as the resolution source for this market (https://apps.apple.com/us/charts/iphone).

[mkt_678925_doc_008] and [mkt_678925_doc_011] discuss ranking factors such as downloads, ratings, reviews, conversion, and revenue, but they are generic algorithm explanations and do not show App H currently has the needed velocity. question:
Will the Senate pass funding bill by October 2?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if both the US Senate pass a government funding bill by October 2, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the US Government. However, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

label: YES
rationale: Base rate for a Senate funding-bill passage by a near-term deadline is somewhat below 0.5, but the evidence is direct and dated. The House passed H.R. 5371, a clean continuing resolution, on Sept. 19, 2025, intended to extend funding until Nov. 21 [mkt_617770_doc_010]. The bill text is already available and is the vehicle for keeping the government funded [mkt_617770_doc_008]. Coverage also indicates the Senate is scheduled to be in session on Sept. 29 and 30 right before the Oct. 2 deadline, making action procedurally feasible [mkt_617770_doc_012]. A Senate committee news item shows appropriations activity is actively moving in FY2026 [mkt_617770_doc_014]. Although the evidence does not prove final Senate passage has already occurred, the combination of a passed House CR, an identified funding vehicle, and imminent Senate session days makes passage by Oct. 2 highly likely [mkt_617770_doc_010] [mkt_617770_doc_012].

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

question:
Will Trump send the national guard to Chicago by December 31?

Description: This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump or his administration takes a formal action or makes an official announcement directing the deployment of National Guard troops to the listed city by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No.”
A qualifying announcement or action will count, regardless of if/when the deployment actually occurs, or if the deployment is delayed, suspended, or subsequently blocked by judicial or other actions.

Qualifying actions include but are not limited to:
- A Presidential action (e.g., Executive Order, Presidential Memorandum) that directs or announces National Guard deployment to the listed city.
- An official White House/DoD/National Guard Bureau press release, fact sheet, or order explicitly stating that National Guard troops will be deployed to the listed city.
- A Secretary of Defense or NGB directive/order that calls up or assigns National Guard forces to the listed city, including under Title 32 (state control with federal funding) or Title 10 (federalized).
- An official federal announcement that specific state Guard units (including the listed city National Guard or out-of-state Guard) are being sent to the listed city.

Non-qualifying actions include:
- Informal announcements/social media posts which do not constitute a formal policy announcement 
- Ambiguous remarks, “we’re considering it,” broad statements like “ready to go anywhere,” or contingent plans without a clear directive to the listed city.
- Generic posture changes (e.g., standing up quick-reaction forces “nationwide”) that do not explicitly name the listed city as a deployment location.
- A state-only activation by the listed city's state Governor without any official Trump administration action or announcement about sending the Guard to the listed city.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question:
Will the highest temperature in London be between 54-55°F on October 26?

Description: This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the London City Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 26 Oct '25.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the London City Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/gb/london/EGLC.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

question:
Will Israel strike Gaza on November 24?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if Israel initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Gaza soil on the listed date Israel Standard Time (IST). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by Israeli military forces that impact Gaza ground territory.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory of the Gaza Strip counts, including buffer zones.

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Gaza territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by Israeli ground operatives will not qualify.

The primary resolution source will be official government/military statements (Israeli or foreign), multilateral bodies (UN, etc.), or a consensus of credible reporting from major international media and national broadcasters/newspapers.

If the date/time of a strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting within 48 hours of this market's end date, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

question:
Will Driver C get pole position at the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix?

Description: This is a polymarket on the driver who achieves pole position at the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix, scheduled for Nov 29, 2025.

If the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after Dec 6, 2025, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the driver who is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2025 F1 Qatar Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a driver sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that driver.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

Resolution source: https://www.formula1.com/en/results/2025/races

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

question:

Description: This market will resolve according to how much "Predator: Badlands" Opening Weekend Box Office will gross domestically on its opening weekend. The "Daily Box Office Performance" figures found on the “Box Office” tab on this movie's The Numbers (https://www.the-numbers.com/) page will be used to resolve this market once the values for the 3-day opening weekend (November 7 - November 9) are final (i.e., not studio estimates).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note, this market will resolve according to the The Numbers figures provided under Weekend Box Office Performance for the 3-day weekend (which typically includes Thursday's previews), regardless of whether domestic refers to only the USA, or to USA and Canada, etc.

If there is ambiguity as to whether the resolution source's figures are final, this market will remain open until both https://www.boxofficemojo.com/ and https://www.the-numbers.com/ have confirmed their finalized figures.

Resolution source: https://www.the-numbers.com/

label: YES

[mkt_663755_doc_007] says Variety’s opening projection is $25M-$30M, again putting the line inside the expected range. [mkt_663755_doc_001] adds that Boxoffice Pro’s long-range forecast was also upbeat, helped by the franchise’s theatrical appeal and comparisons to Alien: Romulus, which opened to $42M. Offsetting that, there is no final box office data yet and the evidence is all pre-release tracking, so I shrink somewhat toward the base rate rather than near-certainty.
